No Poverty.reason: The course explicitly discusses 'income distribution' and 'social welfare' impacts of trade policies, directly linking to Target 1.b on pro-poor policy frameworks and trade policies for developing countries.
evidence: trade policies implemented by developing countries

Zero Hunger.reason: The course's focus on 'tariffs, quotas, export subsidies' and the 'world trade system' directly relates to Target 2.b concerning trade restrictions and distortions in world agricultural markets.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The 'world trade system' and WTO rules, covered in the course, tangentially relate to intellectual property rights (TRIPS) and access to medicines (Target 3.b), but it's not a primary focus.

Quality Education.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to quality education.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course fundamentally analyzes how trade policies impact 'domestic production,' 'prices and quantities of trade,' and 'economic effects,' directly supporting economic growth (Target 8.1) and productive activities (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course examines trade policy tools like 'tariffs and quotas' and their impact on 'domestic production,' which are central to industrialization strategies and Target 9.2.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly states it will analyze the effects of trade on 'income distribution,' making it highly relevant to Targets 10.1 and 10.4. Discussion of the 'world trade system' also links to Target 10.a.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course analyzes 'domestic consumption' and 'domestic production,' which are foundational elements influencing consumption/production patterns, even without explicit mention of sustainability.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: The course's discussion of 'export subsidies' and the 'world trade system' (WTO) is directly relevant to Target 14.6 on prohibiting harmful fisheries subsidies.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence found in the course syllabus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course analyzes the 'world trade system' and 'WTO rules,' which are key institutions of global governance, directly linking to Targets 16.6 and 16.8 on effective institutions and participation in global governance.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is fundamentally about the 'world trade system,' 'WTO rules,' and 'regional trade agreements,' directly addressing Target 17.10 on promoting a universal, rules-based multilateral trading system.
